parties: Rev. Harold L. Webb & Rev. Thomas Miller v. Pacific Presbytery
disposition: dismissed

parties: Session of Trinity Presbyterian Church, Slidell, Louisiana v. Grace Presbytery
disposition: Case returned to lower court with instructions for new hearing; inadequate record

parties: Session of Eastland Presbyterian Church, Memphis, Tennessee v. Covenant Presbytery
disposition: Complaint found invalid; McKeown was not employed in ministerial capacity

parties: Rev. Paul L. Karlberg and Ruling Elder James G. Luce v. Central Georgia Presbytery
disposition: Complaint not sustained

parties: Rev. [REDACTED] (Kim) v. Presbytery of Texas
disposition: Case deemed abandoned

parties: Rev. Thomas Barnes et al v. Presbytery of Evangel
disposition: Complaint not sustained

parties: James H. Campbell v. Mid-Atlantic Presbytery
disposition: General Assembly instructed Mid-Atlantic Presbytery to schedule new hearing on original charges brought by Ruling Elders of New Covenant Presbyterian Church, Virginia Beach, Virginia, against Rev. Robert Schoof; judicial commission appointed for potential further appeal

parties: Luther L. Allin, Jr., et al. (members of Session of Westminster Presbyterian Church) v. Tennessee Valley Presbytery
disposition: Specifications of error sustained regarding lack of 10-day notice, improper participation by Wallace Bristow and Pastor Vitullo; specification regarding mandate of rotation system denied; matter remanded to Tennessee Valley Presbytery for hearing after proper notice

parties: Ruling Elder James H. Campbell v. Presbytery of the Mid-Atlantic
disposition: Complaint partially sustained; Commission sustained complaint on grounds 1, 2, and 3; Presbytery directed to proceed with trial

parties: Teaching Elders Byron Snapp and James M. Hope v. Calvary Presbytery
disposition: sustained in part (remanded to Presbytery for correction)

parties: Ruling Elder James H. Campbell et al. v. Presbytery of Mid-Atlantic
disposition: dismissed (absent complainant/counsel)

parties: Teaching Elder Carl W. Bogue et al. v. Presbytery of the Ascension
disposition: sustained in part (Count 2 sustained; Count 1 split 10-5; remanded to Presbytery for correction and re-examination)

parties: Ruling Elder Cooke Lewis, et al. v. Grace Presbytery
disposition: Complaint not sustained

parties: Ruling Elder Robert H. Zeigler v. Presbytery of Texas
disposition: Judgment of lower court confirmed

parties: Teaching Elder Rodney King, et al. v. Presbytery of Western Carolinas
disposition: Complaint not sustained

parties: Stephen M. Lee, et al. v. Gulf Coast Presbytery
disposition: Complaint sustained in part; General Assembly instructed Gulf Coast Presbytery to re-examine Raymond Bradford Fell for ordination

parties: Charles H. Davis, et al. v. Presbytery of Central Georgia
disposition: Complaint sustained; General Assembly sustained complaint against Presbytery for failing to sustain Davis's complaint about discipline matter

parties: David H. Bryson, et al. v. Presbytery of Central Georgia
disposition: Complaint sustained; same as Case 2 - General Assembly sustained complaint against Presbytery

parties: Vaughn E. Hathaway, Jr., et al. v. Grace Presbytery
disposition: Complaint not sustained; Grace Presbytery acted properly in refusing to request General Assembly assume original jurisdiction in G. Aiken Taylor matter (conditionally directed Western Carolinas Presbytery to proceed)

parties: James K. Workman, et al. (Session of Peace Presbyterian Church) v. Eastern Carolina Presbytery
disposition: Complaint sustained; Eastern Carolina Presbytery's resolution on total abstinence from alcohol improperly binds conscience

parties: Session of First Presbyterian Church, Stanley, North Carolina v. Central Carolina Presbytery
disposition: Mixed: Items 1, 2, and 5 of complaint sustained; Items 3, 4, and 6 not sustained. Assembly directed Presbytery to receive and hear complaints properly

parties: TE Robert L. Reymond et al. v. Illiana Presbytery
disposition: sustained in all parts

parties: RE Val H. Barleman et al. v. Presbytery of Ascension
disposition: sustained

parties: TE Stark Wilbor et al. v. North Georgia Presbytery
disposition: sustained

parties: "Determined Minority" of New Life Presbyterian Church v. Judicial Commission of Central Carolina Presbytery
disposition: partially sustained

parties: TE Jack Eubanks et al. v. Central Carolina Presbytery
disposition: partially sustained

parties: David Lockwood Jr. and Evan Holyfield v. Central Carolina Presbytery
disposition: not sustained

parties: TE Jack Eubanks and TE Charles Wilson v. Central Carolina Presbytery
disposition: partially sustained

parties: TE W. Ted Smith Jr. v. Central Carolina Presbytery
disposition: not sustained

parties: Session of Village Chapel Presbyterian Church v. Presbytery of Eastern Carolina
disposition: abandoned

parties: TE Donald A. Codling et al. v. Presbytery of Eastern Canada
disposition: sustained in part

parties: Elder Roy J. Wuckovich v. Central Carolina Presbytery
disposition: complaint not sustained; lower court decision confirmed

parties: TE Russell D. Toms and TE William L. Thompson v. Central Florida Presbytery
disposition: specification of error sustained; matter referred back to presbytery for proper procedures

parties: TE Warren Gardner, et al. v. Presbytery of North Georgia
disposition: specification of error not sustained

parties: Session of Faith Presbyterian Church, Akron, Ohio, et al. v. Presbytery of Ascension
disposition: specification of continued non-compliance sustained; constitutional violation not sustained; contumacy not sustained; recommendations for remedial action adopted

parties: TE Carl W. Bogue, et al. v. Presbytery of Ascension
disposition: specifications of continued non-compliance and constitutional violation sustained; contumacy not sustained; recommendations for remedial action adopted

parties: TE Robert C. Peterson, et al. v. Presbytery of Ascension
disposition: specifications of continued non-compliance and constitutional violation sustained; recommendations for remedial action adopted

parties: TE S. Michael Preg, Jr., et al. v. Missouri Presbytery
disposition: sustained (16-0 vote)

parties: Kenneth L. Gentry, Jr. et al. v. Calvary Presbytery
disposition: Sustained - Calvary Presbytery erred in sustaining Mr. Hyer's examination; verdict annulled and remanded to presbytery for pastoral counseling and re-examination

parties: Vaughn E. Hathaway, Jr. et al. v. Grace Presbytery
disposition: Complaint not sustained - Commission voted not to sustain complaint on grounds of no explicit constitutional guidance on divorce and remarriage

parties: RE Henry D. Knight, Jr. v. Palmetto Presbytery
disposition: Mixed - Specifications 1 and 3 sustained, specs 2 and 4 not sustained. Matter remanded to Palmetto Presbytery for new hearing on specs 1 and 3

parties: TE Edward A. Steele III v. Great Lakes Presbytery
disposition: Abandoned - Case found abandoned as no one appeared to represent the appeal

parties: RE D. Keith Abee et al. v. James River Presbytery
disposition: All six specifications sustained on grounds of procedural deficiencies. Censures reversed and remanded to presbytery

parties: TE Carl W. Bogue et al. v. Presbytery of the Ascension
disposition: All five specifications sustained - Presbytery instructed to reexamine TE Lutjens and comply with prior General Assembly directives

parties: TE Eric Dye et al. v. Missouri Presbytery
disposition: Specs 1, 2, 4, and 6 sustained. Trial annulled and remanded to Missouri Presbytery for new trial with specific procedural requirements

parties: TE Erwin Morrison, TE Donald Stone, TE David Brewer, TE Steve Christian, RE John Harley, RE David Lachman, RE George Harris v. Philadelphia Presbytery
disposition: Specifications sustained (13-0, 11-1-1, 13-0). Complaint upheld. Directed Philadelphia Presbytery to find temporary system of government for Church Without Walls conforming to BCO. Mr. Porter's ordination not annulled but to be brought into full conformity with BCO provisions through proper ecclesiastical connections.

parties: Carl Fox v. Mississippi Valley Presbytery et al.
disposition: sustained in part; complaints #1, #3, #4 sustained and remanded to presbytery for rectification; complaint #2 neither sustained nor denied

parties: RE David C. Lachman et al. v. Philadelphia Presbytery
disposition: sustained on all specifications; annulled action of Philadelphia Presbytery and instructed presbytery to advise session that physical cruelty does not constitute biblical grounds for divorce

parties: TE Robert G. Rayburn et al. v. Missouri Presbytery
disposition: complaint not sustained; decision of lower court confirmed by vote of 7-4

parties: RE Robert Jamison v. Western Carolina Presbytery
disposition: complaint not sustained; decision of lower court confirmed by vote of 13-0 with 1 abstention

parties: TE John Neville v. Western Carolina Presbytery
disposition: withdrawn

parties: TE James Serio et al. v. Palmetto Presbytery
disposition: sustained; annulled presbytery's action of licensing John Tomlinson and directed presbytery to re-examine him in theology

parties: Complaint regarding Christ Reformed Presbyterian Church, Oreland judicial commission procedures v. Philadelphia Presbytery
disposition: Complaint denied; decision of Philadelphia Presbytery confirmed

parties: Philadelphia Presbytery (regarding Christ Church, Oreland transfer of membership) - Case #4
disposition: Presbytery's instruction to transfer members annulled; debate defect found not sustained; unconstitutional action sustained

parties: Complainants (minority of Pastoral Commission) v. Philadelphia Presbytery (regarding Christ Church, Oreland judicial commission report) - Case #6
disposition: Philadelphia Presbytery's adoption of Judicial Commission report annulled; trials de novo held with guilty verdicts and censures imposed

parties: Three former Ruling Elders v. Philadelphia Presbytery (regarding improper complaint reception and member transfer) - Case #9
disposition: Presbytery's actions on March 12, 1988 annulled; found to have improperly received complaint and violated separation of powers

parties: D. Stine (a member) v. Southern Florida Presbytery (regarding Sabbath recreation activities)
disposition: Presbytery's decision confirmed; complaint denied. Commission votes: Complaint (1) sustained 6, not sustained 13, abstain 1; Complaint (2) sustained 6, not sustained 14.

parties: Session of Grace PCA, Germantown, TN v. Covenant Presbytery (regarding ordination procedures for Associate Pastor)
disposition: Lower court decision confirmed; complaint not sustained. Specification of error: That Covenant Presbytery erred in failing to examine Curtis Crenshaw for installation as Associate Pastor without TE ordination - found NOT sustained.

parties: Session of First PCA, Rowlett, TX v. North Texas Presbytery (regarding minister's view on divorce/remarriage)
disposition: Record found defective (incomplete); case referred to Standing Judicial Commission for adjudication between 17th and 18th General Assemblies

parties: First Presbyterian Church of Rowlett, Texas v. North Texas Presbytery
disposition: complaint dismissed / not sustained; presbytery's action affirmed

parties: First Presbyterian Church of Rowlett, Texas v. North Texas Presbytery
disposition: appeal abandoned / dismissed; presbytery's judgment affirmed
